title: E7.2 Stub-callback session: drive C++ decompiler from Rust
description: Launch Ghidra's decompile binary as a child process. Speak the IPC protocol (newline-framed). All callbacks return COMMAND_ERROR. Confirm we see real query traffic from the decompiler. This proves the protocol is reproducible before we invest in callback implementations.
notes: 2026-05-19: protocol primitives landed (commit 8f9824b). src/decomp/{wire,nibble,packed}.rs — 73 unit tests. Remaining work for the stub-session MVS: (1) lift pspec/cspec/tspec/coretypes for x86-64 GCC; (2) wire top-level command sender for registerProgram/setAction/decompileAt; (3) element/attr name table for the callback subset; (4) 15 stub callbacks; (5) 4 real callbacks (getbytes, getregister, getregistername, getpcode-via-bridge); (6) child-process spawn + dispatch loop.

title: E7.1 Document the 17 decompiler callbacks
description: Read Ghidra's decompiler C++ source (Ghidra/Features/Decompiler/src/decompile/cpp/*). Document each of the 17 COMMAND_GET* callbacks: arguments, return shape, when invoked. Output: docs/decompiler-protocol.md. This is the spec for E7.2+.
notes: 2026-05-11: bumped P2→P0. Phase 3 is the destination; this spike is the cheapest evidence that the protocol reproduces. Run as research spike in parallel with bridge-era work.
close_reason: Spike complete. docs/decompiler-protocol.md (752 lines) covers: process topology (decompiler is child over stdin/stdout, no handshake), wire format (binary 4-byte aligned bursts + PackedEncode payloads + nibble-encoded raw bytes, NOT XML on the wire), 7 top-level commands host->decompiler, 19 callbacks decompiler->host (canonical count is 19, not 17 — two pairs collapse to one Java handler each which probably caused the 17 framing). Biggest Phase-3 blocker: command_getpcode delegates all instruction decoding to the host — need SLEIGH FFI or bridge-backed cache. MVS for E7.2: only 4 callbacks need real impls (getbytes, getregister, getregistername, getpcode) + 3 top-level commands (registerProgram, setAction, decompileAt); the other 15 callbacks can return empty/default. Spec cites file:line on master throughout.

title: E4.6 Structured MCP error schema
description: Every bridge error → MCP error with {code: enum, message, recoverable: bool, hint: string?, suggested_next_tool: string?}. Let the LLM recover automatically from 'no program loaded' by hinting at 'import'.
notes: 2026-05-11: bumped P2→P0. Tightest agent-feedback-loop lever. Structured errors let agents chain recovery without parsing English.
close_reason: Shipped: src/ipc/error.rs (BridgeError + 9-pattern classifier), wired through BridgeClient and MCP err_result. 9/9 unit tests pass. Agents now receive JSON {code, message, recoverable, hint, suggested_next_tool} on tool errors instead of free-text English. Classifier covers no_program_loaded / program_not_found / function_not_found / symbol_not_found / type_not_found / address_invalid / invalid_args / unknown_command / write_failed / bridge_unreachable / bridge_timeout / transport. CLI error path unchanged (Display preserves raw message).

title: E1.3 README: add fork-takeover banner + update clone URL
description: Top of README: short banner noting this is a hard fork of akiselev/ghidra-cli, actively maintained and diverging. Replace clone URL akiselev → phall1. Don't editorialize about upstream — just state the fork relationship factually.

title: E1.1 Cargo.toml: claim fork ownership
description: Replace authors with phall1 (preserve original attribution in a 'forked-from' note in description if desired). Replace repository URL with https://github.com/phall1/ghidra-cli. Update description to reflect AI-native RE positioning. Touches: Cargo.toml.

title: E1: Fork ownership & identity
description: Scrub upstream (akiselev) identity from package metadata, README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE. Bump version to 0.2.0-dev to signal the fork's new line of development. This is foundational — every release-engineering and crates.io decision downstream depends on this. Done = no akiselev references remain outside historical commit messages, fork-takeover banner is on README, version is 0.2.0-dev.
close_reason: All 8 child issues complete; fork identity fully claimed in v0.2.0-dev metadata, README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE.

title: E8.7 Cut 0.2.0 tag
description: Once E1 complete (fork claimed), E2 complete (observability baseline), and E5 complete (bridge refactor) — cut v0.2.0. This is the first signed, multi-arch release under the phall1 fork. All other E8 work feeds into this tag's artifacts.

title: E8.2 cargo-binstall manifest
description: Add [package.metadata.binstall] section to Cargo.toml so 'cargo binstall ghidra-cli' works. Specify URL template for the multi-arch artifacts.

title: E8.1 Multi-arch release workflow
description: Tag-triggered (v*) GitHub Actions workflow that builds binaries for: macos-arm64, macos-x86_64, linux-x86_64, linux-arm64. Uploads to GitHub Releases. cross or native runners as appropriate.

title: E6.4 ghidra-cli annotate apply
description: Subcommand: ghidra-cli annotate apply --in db.sqlite --project P. For each function in project, look up by hash, if found and project unmodified, apply name/signature/comments. Output diff of what would change with --dry-run.

title: E6.2 SQLite annotation DB schema
description: Schema: annotations(function_hash, project, program, name, signature, return_type, params JSON, comments JSON, renames JSON, updated_at). Use rusqlite. Migration framework so we can evolve the schema.

title: E6.3 ghidra-cli annotate export
description: Subcommand: ghidra-cli annotate export --project P --out db.sqlite. Walks all functions in project, hashes them, writes annotations row per function with current state.

title: E6.1 Function hashing strategy: PCode-based fingerprint
description: Design + prototype a function fingerprint that's stable across compiler flag changes (-O0/-O2, different compilers). PCode-based (after normalization) is the candidate. Output: a write-up + a hashing function on a Ghidra Function -> stable 128-bit ID.

title: E5.5 Audit + enforce transaction wrapping on writes
description: Walk every write handler (symbol_create, comment_set, patch_*, struct_*, etc.) and verify it wraps Ghidra API calls in startTransaction/endTransaction with proper rollback on exception. Add a small static-analysis style check (script) that flags writes outside transactions.

title: E4.5 MCP resources for project state
description: Expose current_project, current_function, recent_errors as MCP resources (rmcp Resource trait). Today the LLM has to call tools to discover state — resources push it implicitly. Saves tool calls and context.
close_reason: Shipped: src/mcp/mod.rs now advertises ServerCapabilities.resources and implements list_resources + read_resource. Three URIs: ghidra://project (path, current_program, programs list), ghidra://program (program_info or {loaded:false} when none open), ghidra://bridge (bridge_info). Agents can now discover project/program/bridge state via resource read instead of spending a tool call on get_program_info/list_programs/get_bridge_info. NoProgramLoaded errors are absorbed and turned into {loaded:false} on the program resource so speculative reads don't punish the agent. Tests: tests/mcp_resources.rs (2 tests, async smoke + URI well-formedness). 32 KiB schema budget still under (resources don't count toward tool schema bytes).

title: E4.2 --mcp-tier=1|2|3 flag
description: Add a tier annotation to each tool (essential / frequent / specialized — matches VISION.md). When 'ghidra-cli mcp --tier=1' is used, only essential tools (~20) are exposed. Default exposes all. Lets users trade tool surface for context budget.

title: E4.3 Per-tool invocation counter
description: Track invocations per MCP tool, persist to ~/.local/share/ghidra-cli/tool-usage.json. 'ghidra-cli stats tools' shows which tools are used vs never. Data for E4.4 and for pruning dead tools.

title: E4.1 Schema-byte budget audit
description: Compute total byte count of all 80 MCP tool schemas (sum of JSON-serialized descriptions + parameter schemas). Print it. Set a budget (start: 32 KiB). Fail CI if total exceeds budget. Schemas ride in every LLM context — they're not free.
close_reason: Shipped: GhidraServer::audit_schemas() returns SchemaAudit{tool_count,total_bytes,entries[name,description_bytes,schema_bytes,total_bytes]}. CI gate via tests/mcp_schema_audit.rs at 32 KiB budget (current footprint 25.5 KiB / 79.7%). Override via MCP_SCHEMA_BUDGET_BYTES env. Top 5 fattest tools today: retype_variable (668B), create_enum (628B), rename_variable (607B), get_pcode_function (556B), set_return_type (536B) — candidates for trim or tier-2 demotion in E4.2.

title: E3.8 Insta golden snapshots for canonical binaries
description: Use insta crate for snapshot tests of decompile / list_functions / list_strings on tests/fixtures/* binaries. Goldens commit to repo. When Ghidra version changes a snapshot, reviewer must approve diff. This is how we lock down 'no silent regression' across Ghidra upgrades.

title: E3.6 Cold-start regression gate (links to E2.7)
description: Duplicate of E2.7 in test terms — once E2.6 emits the cold_start_ms event, this gate consumes it in cargo test. Keep them as separate issues so the bridge work (E2) and the test work (E3) can move independently.

title: E3.3 Ghidra version matrix CI (10.x / 11.x / 12.x)
description: Today CI tests against a single Ghidra version. Add a matrix axis for the latest 10.x / 11.x / 12.x and run the E2E suite against all three. This is how we catch reflection-fallback breakage.

title: E3.1 Property tests on protocol roundtrip
description: proptest strategies for BridgeRequest and BridgeResponse. Property: serialize → deserialize → equal. Run on every PR. Today the protocol is hand-tested through E2E only.

title: E2.6 JVM cold-start measurement event
description: In bridge.rs start_bridge, annotate the analyzeHeadless boot span. On ready, emit a single structured event 'jvm_cold_start' with cold_start_ms, ghidra_version, java_version. This is the input to the regression gate.

title: E2.7 Cold-start regression gate in CI
description: CI step: import a fixed canonical binary from clean state, assert cold_start_ms < threshold (start: 30s, ratchet down). Threshold lives in a checked-in file so the ratchet is reviewable.

title: E2.3 --log-format=json flag
description: Add a global --log-format=human|json flag that switches tracing-subscriber between human and JSON layers. Default human for TTY, JSON when piped (mirror the existing format auto-detection pattern).

title: E2.2 Per-command latency histogram (bucketed)
description: Maintain in-memory rolling histogram per command name. Buckets: 1ms, 10ms, 100ms, 1s, 10s, 60s. Flush to stderr at process exit and on demand. Use hdrhistogram crate.

title: E2.1 Wrap BridgeClient calls in tracing spans
description: Every send_command goes inside a #[tracing::instrument] span with attributes: command name, project hash, byte sizes in/out, status. No more silently fast or silently slow calls.

title: E1.7 Audit .github/workflows for upstream paths
description: Workflows may reference akiselev/ghidra-cli in release URLs, badge URLs, or container registry paths. Walk every workflow yaml, replace any upstream references. Verify release.yml (if present) cuts artifacts under phall1's repo.
close_reason: Audit complete: no upstream URL references found in .github/workflows/*.yml (claude.yml, lint.yml, release.yml, test.yml all clean).

title: E1.5 Version bump to 0.2.0-dev
description: Bump Cargo.toml version from 0.1.9 to 0.2.0-dev across the workspace (root + ilspy-cli if it shares versioning). Update Cargo.lock. Verify cargo build succeeds. The -dev suffix prevents accidental release until E8 cuts the actual 0.2.0 tag.

title: E1.4 CHANGELOG: open 0.2.0 stanza, claim fork takeover
description: Move the Java-bridge migration entries from Unreleased into a [0.2.0] - 2026-xx-xx stanza. Add a top-of-file note: 'Beginning 0.2.0 this project is maintained at phall1/ghidra-cli; the akiselev/ghidra-cli upstream is no longer the canonical source.' Fix compare/release links at bottom to phall1.

title: E1.2 ilspy-cli/Cargo.toml: scrub upstream URL
description: Sub-crate ilspy-cli/Cargo.toml has repository = https://github.com/akiselev/ghidra-cli. Update to phall1 fork URL. Confirm whether ilspy-cli should be a workspace member or its own crate.

title: E8: Release engineering
description: Tag-triggered multi-arch release workflow (macOS arm64+x86_64, Linux x86_64+arm64). cargo-binstall recipe. Homebrew tap at phall1/homebrew-tap. cosign-signed artifacts. SBOM via cargo-cyclonedx. Crates.io publishing path decided as ADR. Gate: 0.2.0 ships once E1+E2+E5 are green.

title: E5: Java bridge refactor
description: GhidraCliBridge.java is 4292 lines and dispatches via switch. Replace with Map<String,Handler> registry, inner classes per category (still one file). JVM-side JUnit tests run from CI. Per-handler JMH bench. Enforce transaction wrapping on every write handler (today by convention). Replace reflection-based version fallbacks with explicit Ghidra-version branches.

title: E3: Test rigor upgrade
description: Property tests on BridgeRequest/Response roundtrip. cargo-fuzz on the response parser. Ghidra version matrix CI (10.x / 11.x / 12.x). 1000-call stress + 8h soak with heap watch. Cold-start regression gate. Insta golden snapshots. Coverage floor in CI. 142 E2E tests is breadth; this epic adds depth.

title: E2: Observability & SLOs
description: Bridge round-trips emit structured tracing spans with latency. Per-command histograms. JSON log mode. Published SLO table per command class (query / analyze / import / patch). 'ghidra-cli debug timings' subcommand for last-N samples. JVM cold-start measurement + regression gate. Today 'tracing' is a dep that emits nothing useful — fix that.

title: E8.6 Crates.io publish path ADR
description: Decision: do we publish to crates.io as 'ghidra-cli'? Upstream never published (name appears free, verify). Trade-offs: visibility vs. lock-in to a name. Write up as ADR in docs/adr/. Gates on E1 complete + name check.

title: E7.8 Callback: COMMAND_GETCOMMENTS
description: Return user annotations at address. Initially empty unless we wire in the annotation DB from E6.

title: E7.5 Callback: COMMAND_GETSYMBOL
description: Look up symbol at address in our symbol table. Return name + type. Decompiler uses this to name variables, recognize stdlib functions, etc.

title: E7.4 Callback: COMMAND_GETBYTES
description: Read bytes at (address, length) from the loaded memory image. First and easiest callback. Implements the decompiler's 'show me what's at this address' query.

title: E7.3 goblin-based x86_64 ELF loader
description: Build a Rust loader that produces: memory image (segments + bytes), symbol table (name → addr), import table (name + library), entry points. Use goblin. x86_64 ELF only for now — PE comes later, ARM later still.

title: E3.4 1000-call stress test (FD + heap)
description: Test: run 1000 sequential queries against a warm bridge, assert FD count stable, JVM heap growth < 50MB. Catches resource leaks that single-call E2E won't see.

title: E3.2 cargo-fuzz target on response parser
description: fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| { let _ = BridgeResponse::from_bytes(data); }). Build + run for 60s in CI; catch panics, slow inputs. The bridge response parser is a security boundary — fuzz it.

title: E1.8 LICENSE: append fork copyright line
description: Project stays GPL-3.0. Add a 'Copyright (c) 2026 phall1 contributors' line beneath the existing copyright (do NOT remove the original). GPL-3.0 requires preserving prior copyright notices.

title: E1.6 Delete docs/plan-prod.md
description: This file is marked historical at the top, references upstream URLs in code blocks (akiselev/ghidra-cli), and describes the pre-Java-bridge architecture. It's actively misleading to anyone exploring the docs. Delete.

title: E7: Phase 3 — Decompiler independence (phased)
description: The moat. C++ decompiler driven by a Rust callback host. Phased: (a) protocol reproduction with stub callbacks, (b) goblin x86_64 ELF loader, (c) top-5 callbacks GETBYTES/GETSYMBOL/GETDATATYPE/GETPCODE/GETCOMMENTS, (d) SLEIGH FFI, (e) 100-binary differential validation vs Java host. Each stage has a kill switch — ship as preview if x86_64 doesn't reach parity.

title: E6: Phase 2 — RE persistence layer (compressed)
description: Cut Phase 2 of VISION.md down to its two real engineering items: function-hash annotation DB (survives recompilation) and semantic function diff for cross-binary transfer. Skip auto-indexing, the 'explain' megacommand, and the RE notebook — those are demos a sufficiently motivated user replicates with prompts.

title: E4: MCP surface quality
description: Audit total schema-byte budget for all 80 tools (they all ride in the LLM's context). Add --mcp-tier=1|2|3 to gate tool exposure. Per-tool invocation counters. Tool-selection eval harness against synthetic RE tasks. Expose project/function state as MCP resources, not tool returns. Structured error schema with code, recoverable bool, and hint.

title: test123
description: probe output
close_reason: probe issue from initial bd setup; not real work

title: E7.12 Decision: ship as preview or replace Java path
description: Once E7.10 produces a divergence number, decide: (a) ship Rust path as opt-in preview while Java stays default, or (b) replace Java path entirely. Decision record in docs/adr/. Criterion: divergence rate + maintenance cost.

title: E7.10 Differential validation harness
description: Decompile 100 x86_64 ELF binaries with both (a) Java-host path and (b) Rust-host path. Diff outputs. Track divergence rate as a metric. Goal: < 5% divergence on top-100 functions across all binaries.

title: E7.9 SLEIGH C++ FFI
description: FFI wrapper around Ghidra's SLEIGH runtime (libsleigh). Load .sla files, disassemble instructions, emit PCode. Use cxx crate. This is the largest single piece of Phase 3 — months.

title: E7.7 Callback: COMMAND_GETPCODE
description: Provide PCode for an instruction. Requires SLEIGH FFI (E7.9) to be available. The decompiler asks for PCode block-by-block during decompilation.

title: E7.6 Callback: COMMAND_GETDATATYPE
description: Resolve type definitions. Start with primitives + pointers + simple structs from DWARF if present. This is where the type system complexity will show up.

title: E6.6 ghidra-cli annotate transfer
description: Subcommand: ghidra-cli annotate transfer --from A --to B. Uses E6.5 mapping to copy annotations from program A to program B in the same project. Confidence threshold flag --min-confidence.

title: E6.5 Semantic function diff (cross-binary mapping)
description: Given two programs A and B, produce a mapping A-functions -> B-functions using PCode-hash + neighbor-in-callgraph heuristic. Output: ranked candidate pairs with confidence.

title: E4.4 Tool-selection eval harness
description: Build a small harness: synthetic RE tasks + expected tool-call sequences → score an LLM's tool selection. Run against Claude / GPT. Track 'tool selection accuracy' as a regression metric when we change tool descriptions or add tools.

title: E8.5 SBOM generation via cargo-cyclonedx
description: Generate CycloneDX SBOM per release, upload as a release artifact. Helps anyone auditing the dep graph.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E8.4 cosign-signed artifacts
description: Sign release artifacts with cosign (keyless via OIDC). Publish .sig + .pem alongside binaries. Verifiable supply chain — modest effort, high signaling value.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E8.3 Homebrew tap phall1/homebrew-tap
description: Create the tap repo. Add a formula for ghidra-cli that points at the released artifacts. Release workflow updates the formula on each tag.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E7.11 PE x86_64 support
description: Once ELF works, add PE x86_64. Mostly: PE loader (goblin handles it) + Windows calling convention. Gated on E7.10 passing for ELF.

title: E5.7 SpotBugs / Error Prone on the Java file
description: Add SpotBugs (or Error Prone) as a CI step on GhidraCliBridge.java. Catches null-deref, resource leak, common bug patterns before they reach review.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E5.6 Explicit Ghidra version branches
description: Today reflection / try-catch fallback handles API differences across Ghidra 10/11/12 (e.g. AutoImporter signature changes). Replace with explicit version detection at startup + branched code paths. Easier to reason about, easier to test, fails loudly when a new Ghidra version breaks.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E5.4 JMH per-handler benchmark
description: JMH harness in bench/java/. Bench each handler in isolation to attribute latency: how much is socket / serialization / Ghidra API. Run nightly, post results as artifact.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E5.3 JVM-side JUnit tests
description: Set up tests/java/ with JUnit 5. Each handler gets at least one unit test that hits the handler directly (not through the socket). Run via CI step that compiles Java sources + runs junit-platform-console-launcher. No gradle.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E5.2 Inner classes per category
description: Group handlers into inner classes: ProgramHandlers, SymbolHandlers, FunctionHandlers, etc. All static inner, same file. Improves navigability without splitting the file or adding build tooling.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E5.1 Replace switch dispatch with handler registry
description: Today handleRequest is a giant switch. Replace with private static final Map<String, Handler> HANDLERS keyed by command name, with a Handler functional interface (args -> JsonObject). Single-file constraint preserved.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E3.7 cargo-llvm-cov in CI with floor
description: Coverage report generation in CI. Set a floor (start at 75% line coverage, ratchet up). Reject PRs that drop below floor. We don't need 100% — we need the floor to prevent silent regression.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E3.5 8h soak test (nightly cron)
description: Nightly CI job: random tool calls for 8 hours against a long-running bridge, JVM heap watched, FD watched, assert no OOM and no leak slope. Output a stability report artifact.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E2.5 ghidra-cli debug timings subcommand
description: Subcommand that dumps the last-N command latencies and per-command histograms from the rolling buffer. Output formats: table + json. Useful both as user-visible perf debug and as CI input.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.

title: E2.4 docs/SLO.md: published SLO table
description: Create docs/SLO.md with: command class definitions (query / analyze / import / patch), p50/p95/p99 latency targets per class, cold-start budget (JVM boot < 15s, first-query < 5s after boot), measurement methodology. Targets are aspirational but committed — CI gates land in E2.7/E3.6.
notes: 2026-05-11: deferred to backlog. JVM-era polish; this work hardens the Java bridge or release pipeline that will be replaced by Phase 3. Revisit only if Phase 3 stalls past 2026-09.
